subject: [POLICY] certInit support + refactoring
commit_message:
[POLICY] certInit support + refactoring

Several changes are including in this patch:
- certInitializer support (POLICY-2615, REQ-361)
- renamed policy objects to start with "policy-"
  prefix (POLICY-1000)
- add resources section to components that did not
  have or had it incorrectly set up rendering null
  (POLICY-2502)
- Removal of legacy policy-engine components (POLICY-2743)
- Miscellaneous refactoring of charts (POLICY-2745)
- update pdp legacy reference to policy-xacml-pdp from
  pdp (legacy)

Issue-ID: POLICY-2615
Signed-off-by: jhh <jorge.hernandez-herrero@att.com>
Change-Id: I8b6984a663bbb14d331a366ec02b6dd38755cde7
Signed-off-by: jhh <jorge.hernandez-herrero@att.com>

Patch Set 3:

@Krzysztof, @Sylvain, @Morgan, the changes work well in my install and passes healthchecks.   In this review though, when oom_redeploy completes, the expectation is that it will only pass 1 of the 2 healthchecks, it will fail in the "drools" one, as the service has been changed to "policy-drools-pdp" to add "policy" prefix and "-pdp" naming conventions.    So, the passing of the full healthcheck depends on this other review in the testsuite/oom repo:

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/testsuite/oom/+/111070

Not sure how you would want to handle it in order to see the both policy healthchecks passing in the report generated here.

Good to open it, but not sure if there's work on portal side, as in Frankfurt release, per PORTAL-893 ticket, policy gui was discontinued to be accessed through Portal (gui access was standalone).

@Jorge, When I did helm deploy, built with this patch, I got the following errors:

fetching local/onap
Release "dev-policy" does not exist. Installing it now.
Error: release dev-policy failed: secrets "dev-policy-db-secret" already exists

my command was "helm deploy dev-policy local/onap -f /root/oom/kubernetes/onap/resources/environments/public-cloud.yaml -f /root/integration-override.yaml --namespace onap --verbose"

do I need to clean up the cache under deploy plugin? not sure how to fix it.
